content.problemStatement:
You have NNN sticks, of integral lengths A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.

You can take any stick of length XXX and break it into 222 **positive integer** lengths YYY and ZZZ such that Y+Z=XY + Z = XY+Z=X, i.e. now instead of the stick of length XXX, you obtained one stick of length YYY and another of length ZZZ.

One such action is called a **break**. Find the maximum number of **breaks** you can perform.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of input contains NNN - the number of sticks.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​, the lengths of the sticks




Output Format
For each test case, find the maximum number of **breaks** you can perform.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are no possible breaks to do because it is not possible to split 111 into 222 positive integer numbers.
Test Case 2 : We can break the stick of length 333 into sticks of lengths 222 and 111. Further, we then break the stick of length 222 into sticks of lengths 111 and 111. Thus, we performed 222 breaks.
